contact.name: Laguna Beach Tennis Academy
contact.primary_tagline: Tennis, as it should be taught.
contact.city_partner_line: Official City of Laguna Beach Tennis Partner Since 2020

coaches.name: Andrew Mateljan
coaches.title: Founder & Director of Tennis
coaches.specialization: Founder & Director of Tennis · Head Coach, LBHS Boys Varsity Tennis
coaches.bio: Fourth-generation tennis coach. Twenty years developing competitive players from junior foundations to professional tour. Top-12 nationally as a junior, two-time Kalamazoo Nationals competitor, ITF Futures circuit (US, Venezuela, El Salvador, Guatemala). Trained at Sánchez-Casal Spain under Jimmy Johnson; seven years coaching internationally across Spain, Croatia, and Norway. Currently coaches Karue Sell (ATP #258 — took him from #860 to #258 in 2025). Colton Smith (ATP #133), Max McKennon (#458), and Ryan Seggerman (#63 in doubles) all came through the same training. Alex Michelsen came through the program at ages 12–13 — now ATP #30.

coaches.name: Peter DeFrantz
coaches.title: Head Coach, Junior Development
coaches.specialization: USPTA & PTR Certified Professional · College Tennis at Mt. San Jacinto · 8+ Years Coaching
coaches.bio: Peter grew up on the tennis court — his father coached high school tennis — and learned early that the game is about more than strokes: how you carry yourself, compete, and treat people. College tennis at Mt. San Jacinto College; dual USPTA and PTR certified. Eight years coaching across Southern California with a progressive, player-centered approach.
coaches.quote: Build the player, not just the game.

coaches.name: Allison Cronk
coaches.title: Head Coach, Youth Programs
coaches.specialization: Head Coach, Youth Programs · Collegiate Player · Educator
coaches.bio: Allison played varsity tennis at Mira Costa High School, then competed at Westcliff University (NAIA) and Shepherd University (NCAA) — Dean's List every semester at both. She has coached at five academies across Orange County and the South Bay. Beginners, juniors, adults, competitors. She adapts to whoever is on the other side of the net.
coaches.quote: She doesn't just teach the game. She teaches the person playing it.

junior.location: Moulton Meadows Park + Saturday at Alta Laguna
junior.description: Your child's first time on a tennis court should feel like play — because it is. Foam balls, low nets, mini courts. Movement games, relay races, treasure hunts. No drills. No pressure.

junior.location: Moulton Meadows Park + Saturday at Alta Laguna
junior.description: The real starting line. Players learn to swing, move, and rally on a 36-foot court with low-compression red balls. By the end of the season, your child is hitting 4–6 ball rallies, serving to start a point, and keeping score.

junior.location: Moulton Meadows Park + Saturday at Alta Laguna
junior.description: Bigger court. Faster ball. Players move to a 60-foot court with orange balls — closer to the real game. Direction becomes a coached focus. Volleys and overhand serves are introduced through games, and structured point play begins.

junior.location: Moulton Meadows Park + Saturday at Alta Laguna
junior.description: Full-size court. Green dot balls — 25% slower than regulation. This is where players learn to think. We introduce Phase Play: is this a rally ball, an attack ball, or a defensive ball?

development.location: Alta Laguna Park (Tue/Thu); Laguna Beach High School (Fri)
development.description: For green dot players who are ready for more than group classes. Match play, point construction, and tournament preparation. Placement by coach invitation.
development.pricing_note: Placement by invitation. Contact us to discuss if your player may be ready.

development.location: Alta Laguna Park (Mon/Wed); Laguna Beach High School (Fri)
development.description: The first competitive training tier. Players develop their own playing style, learn to manage points independently, and build the physical and mental habits that tournament play demands. Two tiers by UTR — Tier 1 (UTR 1–4) and Tier 2 (UTR 4–7).
development.pricing_note: Under 11 but already at UTR 1.5+? Contact us to inquire about placement.

development.description: The highest level of training at LBTA. Every session simulates tournament conditions. UTR 8+ required. Coach approval required.
development.pricing_note: UTR 8+ required. Under 12 or close to UTR 8? Contact us to discuss readiness.

adult.description: No rating required. No experience needed. We get you hitting the ball on day one.
You can rally. You can serve. Now learn to place the ball and start thinking about doubles strategy.

Tennis becomes a tactical sport at this level. The 90-minute format gives 30+ minutes of real match play every session. USTA league preparation begins here. Tue, Thu, and Sat are interchangeable days inside the 1×/2×/3× package — pick any combination.
Full tactical play. Phase Play is assumed. All five ball controls are active. Feeds directly into USTA league teams and UTR competition.

open-court.description: High-energy, coach-fed sessions. 150–200 ball contacts per session. Intermediate (NTRP 3.0–4.0) and Advanced (4.0+) levels. Minimum 5 players to run.
Tennis-based fitness. High-intensity rallies, movement drills, and competitive games — all coach-fed.

leagues.name: Mixed 40+ Doubles
leagues.format: 3 doubles lines, mixed gender, combined NTRP

leagues.name: Adult 55+ Doubles
leagues.format: 3 doubles lines, same gender, combined NTRP

ntrpGuide.desc: Can rally but inconsistent. Learning serve. New to match play.
Consistent rally. Developing serve. Playing points with intention.
Reliable groundstrokes and serve. Can place the ball. Comfortable in doubles.
Strong all-court game. Spin, pace variation, tactical patterns.
Tournament-level. Weapons on serve and groundstrokes. Advanced tactics.

divisions.note: Coach on court for every match. Underhand serves and drop feeds are totally fine — no pressure. If they can rally, they can play.

annual_calendar.scholarship_note: Scholarships: $25,000 awarded annually · 25–50% coverage · email support@lagunabeachtennisacademy.com

pod.name: The Pod
pod.scarcity_line: By invitation. Two paying families per 3-player Pod.
pod.configurations_narrative: Two configurations. A 3-player Pod has two paying families plus Henry. A 4-player Pod has three. The number is fixed; there is no twentieth student waiting for a slot to open.
pod.what_is_included_narrative: One tuition covers everything: on-court training three to five days per week, individual development plans, tournament programming, travel coaching when families travel, and the standing availability you'd expect from a coach who knows your child by name. It is a year-long program commitment, not a per-session fee.

track_record.name: Henry Mateljan
track_record.narrative: Henry Mateljan, age nine, joined the Pod at UTR 2.48. Eighteen months later he is at 4.60, currently in the 4.60–5.00 band.

pod.team_narrative: Andrew runs every Pod session personally. Twenty years coaching internationally and on tour. Took Karue Sell from ATP #860 to #258 in 2025. Trained Colton Smith (#133), Max McKennon (#458), Ryan Seggerman (#63 in doubles). Alex Michelsen came through the program at ages 12–13 and is now ATP #30.
pod.d1_pipeline: Sean Daryabeigi (USC, Blue Chip #2) · Rebecca Lynn (Yale) · Lauren Stein (Cornell)
pod.cta_text: Pod placement is by invitation. To request consideration, write to Andrew directly at andrew@lagunabeachtennisacademy.com. He reads and replies to every Pod inquiry personally.

adult_decision.if: Comfortable rallying, 1+ years, consistent serve · NTRP 3.0–4.0

placement.cta_internal: Internal use: cross-reference with PlayByPoint enrollment. Note: junior programs stay kid-only — no adult drop-ins.
placement.cta_external: Not sure where you (or your player) belong? Reply to support@lagunabeachtennisacademy.com and we'll match you to the right starting point. First session is on us.

concierge.intro: If your guest brought a racquet — or wishes they had — point them here. Laguna Beach Tennis Academy runs the city's official tennis programs across three parks, ten minutes from most hotels. Drop-in clinics, private lessons, and camp drop-in for traveling families.

family.intro: A clear pathway from age three through college tennis — same coaches, same courts, same standard. Start with a $50 LiveBall or a complimentary trial.

warm_lead.intro: Thanks for getting in touch. This is a quick map of how we train at LBTA — the pathway, the coaches, and the programs that fit where you (or your player) are right now. Reply to this email and I'll match you to the right starting point.

private.intro: Private coaching at LBTA is built around your goals: technical clarity, match preparation, return-to-play after injury, or a structured ramp toward competitive tennis. Three coaches with published rates and 10/20-pack savings. Sessions on city-managed courts — no club membership required.
